description_long: This object is a vintage corded telephone with a robust, boxy base made of dark, polished metal, giving it a heavy and durable appearance. The base features subtle metallic patina and a stepped plinth, suggesting an early 20th-century design. Atop the base sits a central post supporting a horizontal cylindrical handset, which is connected to the base by a thin, straight wire. The handset has two rounded earpieces at either end, and the cradle is integrated into the central post, allowing the handset to rest securely. There are no visible buttons or rotary dial on the exterior, indicating a minimalist or decorative style. The overall finish is glossy with a deep bronze or brass coloration, and the object exudes a sense of historical elegance.
description: A vintage, dark metallic corded telephone with a boxy base, glossy finish, and a cylindrical handset resting on a central cradle. The design is heavy, ornate, and lacks visible dialing mechanisms.
description_view_0: Front view shows the solid, stepped base and the central post with the handset resting on top, no visible controls or dials.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the cylindrical handset, the wire connecting it to the base, and the cradle structure; a small protruding lever is visible.
description_view_2: Rear view highlights the plain back of the base and the central post, with the handset's ends visible but no additional features.
description_view_3: Opposite side view shows the handset, wire, and another small lever, mirroring the features seen in view 1.
